creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
okay, I went ahead and created a "fast" conversion to UTF-8 (from ISO8859-1) and all the hashtable problems cleared up.  However, the ports that had null pointers in hash table values also suffer from the CS8 problem, e.g. 

Error occurred during initialization of VM
java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong.VMSupportsCS8()Z
        at java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong.VMSupportsCS8(Native Method)
        at java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong.<clinit>(AtomicLong.java:65)
        at java.lang.reflect.Proxy$ProxyClassFactory.<clinit>(Proxy.java:599)
        at java.lang.reflect.Proxy.<clinit>(Proxy.java:237)
        at sun.reflect.misc.ReflectUtil.isNonPublicProxyClass(ReflectUtil.java:287)
        at sun.reflect.misc.ReflectUtil.checkPackageAccess(ReflectUtil.java:165)
        at java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReferenceFieldUpdater$AtomicReferenceFieldUpdaterImpl.<init>(AtomicReferenceFieldUpdater.java:216)
        at java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReferenceFieldUpdater.newUpdater(AtomicReferenceFieldUpdater.java:95)
        at java.io.BufferedInputStream.<clinit>(BufferedInputStream.java:69)
        at java.lang.System.initializeSystemClass(System.java:1144)

The current version that includes the fast conversion is here: https://www.dragonflybsd.org/~marino/patch-fix-openjdk7-locales_2.txt

When the CS8 problem is fixed, maybe all these ports build again.
